title: quercus ilex subvar. parvifolia
intro: Quercus ilex subvar. parvifolia, also known as the small-leaved holm oak or small-leaved evergreen oak, is a subspecies of the holm oak tree (Quercus ilex) native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Quercus ilex subvar. parvifolia include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and prevent disease.
